title: fix(polly): extract image_url parts from openai content arrays
author: fisventurous
pr_body_excerpt:
fixes polly's HTTP API endpoint silently dropping images from openai-format content arrays

the `/v1/chat/completions` endpoint parsed list-type `content` for text parts but discarded `image_url` entries entirely. other providers (openai, claude, gemini) handle the same image fine because they receive the full content array. polly's API was the only path stripping them.

changes
\- extract `image_url` parts from list-type message content alongside text
\- merge extracted URLs with the explicit `image_urls` field before passing to `process_with_tools`
\- discord path was unaffected (uses `extract_media_urls` on attachments separately)

ref #11669

gist.headline: Polly gets her glasses back
gist.blurb: Fixed a sneaky bug where Polly's API was ignoring images sent in OpenAI-format content arrays. Now she can see everything you show her!
gist.summary: Fixed an issue in the /v1/chat/completions endpoint where image_url entries in OpenAI-format content arrays were being silently discarded.
gist.impact: Developers sending multimodal requests with image URLs inside the content array will now have their images successfully processed by Polly.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing cute reading glasses, successfully looking at a photograph held by the Monitor Robot, symbolizing restored vision capabilities for the API.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing tiny cute reading glasses, looking happily at a pixelated photograph held up by the chunky monitor robot. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, chunky sprites, emotionally warm.
